lines.gurmukhi: ਇਸਤ੍ਰੀ ਪੁਰਖੈ ਜਾਂ ਨਿਸਿ ਮੇਲਾ ਓਥੈ ਮੰਧੁ ਕਮਾਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: eisatree purakhai jaan nis melaa othai mandh kamaahee |
lines.english: But when men and women meet in the night, they come together in the flesh.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਸਹੁ ਨਿੰਮੇ ਮਾਸਹੁ ਜੰਮੇ ਹਮ ਮਾਸੈ ਕੇ ਭਾਂਡੇ ॥
lines.translit: maasahu ninme maasahu jame ham maasai ke bhaandde |
lines.english: In the flesh we are conceived, and in the flesh we are born; we are vessels of flesh.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਹਰ ਕਾ ਮਾਸੁ ਮੰਦਾ ਸੁਆਮੀ ਘਰ ਕਾ ਮਾਸੁ ਚੰਗੇਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: baahar kaa maas mandaa suaamee ghar kaa maas changeraa |
lines.english: O master, you believe that flesh on the outside is bad, but the flesh of those in your own home is good.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਿਆਨੁ ਧਿਆਨੁ ਕਛੁ ਸੂਝੈ ਨਾਹੀ ਚਤੁਰੁ ਕਹਾਵੈ ਪਾਂਡੇ ॥
lines.translit: giaan dhiaan kachh soojhai naahee chatur kahaavai paandde |
lines.english: You know nothing of spiritual wisdom and meditation, even though you call yourself clever, O religious scholar.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੀਅ ਜੰਤ ਸਭਿ ਮਾਸਹੁ ਹੋਏ ਜੀਇ ਲਇਆ ਵਾਸੇਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: jeea jant sabh maasahu hoe jee leaa vaaseraa |
lines.english: All beings and creatures are flesh; the soul has taken up its home in the flesh.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਭਖੁ ਭਖਹਿ ਭਖੁ ਤਜਿ ਛੋਡਹਿ ਅੰਧੁ ਗੁਰੂ ਜਿਨ ਕੇਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: abhakh bhakheh bhakh taj chhoddeh andh guroo jin keraa |
lines.english: They eat the uneatable; they reject and abandon what they could eat. They have a teacher who is blind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਸਹੁ ਨਿੰਮੇ ਮਾਸਹੁ ਜੰਮੇ ਹਮ ਮਾਸੈ ਕੇ ਭਾਂਡੇ ॥
lines.translit: maasahu ninme maasahu jame ham maasai ke bhaandde |
lines.english: In the flesh we are conceived, and in the flesh we are born; we are vessels of flesh.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਸੁ ਪੁਰਾਣੀ ਮਾਸੁ ਕਤੇਬਂ‍ੀ ਚਹੁ ਜੁਗਿ ਮਾਸੁ ਕਮਾਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: maas puraanee maas katebanee chahu jug maas kamaanaa |
lines.english: Meat is allowed in the Puraanas, meat is allowed in the Bible and the Koran. Throughout the four ages, meat has been used.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਿਆਨੁ ਧਿਆਨੁ ਕਛੁ ਸੂਝੈ ਨਾਹੀ ਚਤੁਰੁ ਕਹਾਵੈ ਪਾਂਡੇ ॥
lines.translit: giaan dhiaan kachh soojhai naahee chatur kahaavai paandde |
lines.english: You know nothing of spiritual wisdom and meditation, even though you call yourself clever, O religious scholar.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਸਤ੍ਰੀ ਪੁਰਖ ਨਿਪਜਹਿ ਮਾਸਹੁ ਪਾਤਿਸਾਹ ਸੁਲਤਾਨਾਂ ॥
lines.translit: eisatree purakh nipajeh maasahu paatisaah sulataanaan |
lines.english: Women, men, kings and emperors originate from meat.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਜਿ ਕਾਜਿ ਵੀਆਹਿ ਸੁਹਾਵੈ ਓਥੈ ਮਾਸੁ ਸਮਾਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: jaj kaaj veeaeh suhaavai othai maas samaanaa |
lines.english: It is featured in sacred feasts and marriage festivities; meat is used in them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਓਇ ਦਿਸਹਿ ਨਰਕਿ ਜਾਂਦੇ ਤਾਂ ਉਨੑ ਕਾ ਦਾਨੁ ਨ ਲੈਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: je oe diseh narak jaande taan una kaa daan na lainaa |
lines.english: If you see them going to hell, then do not accept charitable gifts from them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਂਦਾ ਨਰਕਿ ਸੁਰਗਿ ਲੈਦੇ ਦੇਖਹੁ ਏਹੁ ਧਿਙਾਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: dendaa narak surag laide dekhahu ehu dhingaanaa |
lines.english: The giver goes to hell, while the receiver goes to heaven - look at this injustice.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਿ ਨ ਬੂਝੈ ਲੋਕ ਬੁਝਾਏ ਪਾਂਡੇ ਖਰਾ ਸਿਆਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: aap na boojhai lok bujhaae paandde kharaa siaanaa |
lines.english: You do not understand your own self, but you preach to other people. O Pandit, you are very wise indeed.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਂਡੇ ਤੂ ਜਾਣੈ ਹੀ ਨਾਹੀ ਕਿਥਹੁ ਮਾਸੁ ਉਪੰਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: paandde too jaanai hee naahee kithahu maas upanaa |
lines.english: O Pandit, you do not know where meat originated.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੋਇਅਹੁ ਅੰਨੁ ਕਮਾਦੁ ਕਪਾਹਾਂ ਤੋਇਅਹੁ ਤ੍ਰਿਭਵਣੁ ਗੰਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: toeiahu an kamaad kapaahaan toeiahu tribhavan ganaa |
lines.english: Corn, sugar cane and cotton are produced from water. The three worlds came from water.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੋਆ ਆਖੈ ਹਉ ਬਹੁ ਬਿਧਿ ਹਛਾ ਤੋਐ ਬਹੁਤੁ ਬਿਕਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: toaa aakhai hau bahu bidh hachhaa toai bahut bikaaraa |
lines.english: Water says, "I am good in many ways." But water takes many forms.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਤੇ ਰਸ ਛੋਡਿ ਹੋਵੈ ਸੰਨਿਆਸੀ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਕਹੈ ਵਿਚਾਰਾ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: ete ras chhodd hovai saniaasee naanak kahai vichaaraa |2|
lines.english: Forsaking these delicacies, one becomes a true Sannyaasee, a detached hermit. Nanak reflects and speaks. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਕਿਆ ਆਖਾ ਇਕ ਜੀਭ ਤੇਰਾ ਅੰਤੁ ਨ ਕਿਨ ਹੀ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: hau kiaa aakhaa ik jeebh teraa ant na kin hee paaeaa |
lines.english: What can I say with only one tongue? I cannot find your limits.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚਾ ਸਬਦੁ ਵੀਚਾਰਿ ਸੇ ਤੁਝ ਹੀ ਮਾਹਿ ਸਮਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: sachaa sabad veechaar se tujh hee maeh samaaeaa |
lines.english: Those who contemplate the True Word of the Shabad are absorbed into You, O Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਿ ਭਗਵਾ ਵੇਸੁ ਕਰਿ ਭਰਮਦੇ ਵਿਣੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕਿਨੈ ਨ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: eik bhagavaa ves kar bharamade vin satigur kinai na paaeaa |
lines.english: Some wander around in saffron robes, but without the True Guru, no one finds the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਸ ਦਿਸੰਤਰ ਭਵਿ ਥਕੇ ਤੁਧੁ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਆਪੁ ਲੁਕਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: des disantar bhav thake tudh andar aap lukaaeaa |
lines.english: They wander in foreign lands and countries until they grow weary, but You hide Yourself within them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕਾ ਸਬਦੁ ਰਤੰਨੁ ਹੈ ਕਰਿ ਚਾਨਣੁ ਆਪਿ ਦਿਖਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: gur kaa sabad ratan hai kar chaanan aap dikhaaeaa |
lines.english: The Word of the Guru's Shabad is a jewel, through which the Lord shines forth and reveals Himself.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਣਾ ਆਪੁ ਪਛਾਣਿਆ ਗੁਰਮਤੀ ਸਚਿ ਸਮਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: aapanaa aap pachhaaniaa guramatee sach samaaeaa |
lines.english: Realizing one's own self, following the Guru's Teachings, the mortal is absorbed into Truth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਵਾ ਗਉਣੁ ਬਜਾਰੀਆ ਬਾਜਾਰੁ ਜਿਨੀ ਰਚਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: aavaa gaun bajaareea baajaar jinee rachaaeaa |
lines.english: Coming and going, the tricksters and magicians put on their magic show.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕੁ ਥਿਰੁ ਸਚਾ ਸਾਲਾਹਣਾ ਜਿਨ ਮਨਿ ਸਚਾ ਭਾਇਆ ॥੨੫॥
lines.translit: eik thir sachaa saalaahanaa jin man sachaa bhaaeaa |25|
lines.english: But those whose minds are pleased by the True Lord, praise the True One, the Ever-stable Lord. ||25||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਮਾਇਆ ਕਰਮ ਬਿਰਖੁ ਫਲ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਫਲ ਵਿਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: naanak maaeaa karam birakh fal amrit fal vis |
lines.english: O Nanak, the tree of actions done in Maya yields ambrosial fruit and poisonous fruit.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭ ਕਾਰਣ ਕਰਤਾ ਕਰੇ ਜਿਸੁ ਖਵਾਲੇ ਤਿਸੁ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: sabh kaaran karataa kare jis khavaale tis |1|
lines.english: The Creator does all deeds; we eat the fruits as He ordains. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਦੁਨੀਆ ਕੀਆਂ ਵਡਿਆਈਆਂ ਅਗੀ ਸੇਤੀ ਜਾਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: naanak duneea keean vaddiaaeean agee setee jaal |
lines.english: O Nanak, burn worldly greatness and glory in the fire.

lines.translit: mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਨੀ ਜਲੀਈਂ ਨਾਮੁ ਵਿਸਾਰਿਆ ਇਕ ਨ ਚਲੀਆ ਨਾਲਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: enee jaleeeen naam visaariaa ik na chaleea naal |2|
lines.english: These burnt offerings have caused mortals to forget the Naam, the Name of the Lord. Not even one of them will go along with you in the end. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਲੁ ਚਲਾਏ ਬੰਨਿ ਕੋਇ ਨ ਰਖਸੀ ॥
lines.translit: kaal chalaae ban koe na rakhasee |
lines.english: The mortal is bound and gagged by Death and lead away; no one can rescue him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਿਰਿ ਸਿਰਿ ਹੋਇ ਨਿਬੇੜੁ ਹੁਕਮਿ ਚਲਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: sir sir hoe niberr hukam chalaaeaa |
lines.english: He judges each and every being; by the Hukam of His Command, He leads us on.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੇਰੈ ਹਥਿ ਨਿਬੇੜੁ ਤੂਹੈ ਮਨਿ ਭਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: terai hath niberr toohai man bhaaeaa |
lines.english: Justice is in Your Hands, O Lord; You are pleasing to my mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਬੋਹਿਥੁ ਬੇੜੁ ਸਚਾ ਰਖਸੀ ॥
lines.translit: satigur bohith berr sachaa rakhasee |
lines.english: So climb aboard the boat of the True Guru, and the True Lord will rescue you.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਰੁ ਜਰਵਾਣਾ ਕੰਨੑਿ ਚੜਿਆ ਨਚਸੀ ॥
lines.translit: jar jaravaanaa kana charriaa nachasee |
lines.english: Old age, the tyrant, dances on the mortal's shoulders.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਗਨਿ ਭਖੈ ਭੜਹਾੜੁ ਅਨਦਿਨੁ ਭਖਸੀ ॥
lines.translit: agan bhakhai bharrahaarr anadin bhakhasee |
lines.english: The fire of desire burns like an oven, consuming mortals night and day.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਤਾ ਕਰੇ ਸੁ ਹੋਗੁ ਕੂੜੁ ਨਿਖੁਟਸੀ ॥੨੬॥
lines.translit: karataa kare su hog koorr nikhuttasee |26|
lines.english: Whatever the Creator does, comes to pass; falsehood shall fail in the end. ||26||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਾਥਾ ਚੁਗੈ ਚੋਗ ਹੁਕਮੀ ਛੁਟਸੀ ॥
lines.translit: faathaa chugai chog hukamee chhuttasee |
lines.english: Like trapped birds, the mortals peck at the corn; only through the Lord's Command will they find release.
